title: Hello, GCP
description: Every type inferred from zero annotations – welcome to GCP.

title: String & Escape Sequences
description: String concatenation, escape sequences (\n \t \") — all decoded at parse time.

title: Numeric Tower
description: GCP infers the narrowest numeric type automatically.

title: Curried Functions
description: All Outline functions are curried — GCP infers the full A→B→C chain.

title: Higher-Order Functions
description: Functions that accept or return functions — GCP tracks the full type chain.

title: Function Composition
description: Compose two functions — GCP infers the full composed type chain.

title: Recursive Functions
description: GCP handles self-referential let bindings — factorial and GCD.

title: Sum Types (ADT)
description: GCP infers the precise ADT variant for every match branch.

title: Entity Types (Product Types)
description: Struct-like entity types with typed fields — GCP infers field access types.

title: Literal Types
description: Literal types pin a field to a fixed constant — the value is part of the type and is auto-filled at construction.

title: Pattern Matching + Guards
description: Exhaustive match with guard conditions — GCP infers a unified result type.

title: Nested Entity Access
description: Deep-nested anonymous entity literals — GCP resolves each field accessor chain.

title: Arrays & Pipelines
description: Ranges, map, filter, reduce — all inferred end-to-end. Note: reduce takes combiner first, then initial value.

title: Data Pipeline
description: A realistic data-processing pipeline — filter, map, reduce all chained.

title: ⚡ GCP Inference Showcase
description: Generics, HOFs, ADTs and closures — GCP infers it all with zero annotations.

title: Generic Functions
description: GCP propagates generic type variables through arbitrary call chains.

title: ★ Structural Type Synthesis
description: GCP synthesises the complete entity type from HOW functions use it — zero declarations, zero annotations.

title: ★ Backward Constraint Propagation
description: Type flows BACKWARD through a HOF chain: the entity type is inferred from how the selector uses it, not from the entity itself.

title: ★ Deep HOF Error Detection
description: GCP traces a missing field through nested HOF composition — error caught at call site, not inside the combinator.

title: ★ Entity Templates: Defaults & Literals
description: Default-value fields auto-fill but can be overridden; literal-type fields are constant forever. Both are absent from TypeScript and Haskell without boilerplate.

title: ★ Literal Types as Constant Fields
description: GCP literal types ARE the value — conforming entities auto-fill the field without ever assigning it. TypeScript literal types are constraints only; Haskell has no equivalent.

title: ★ Constraint Convergence
description: Two independent functions each constrain a DIFFERENT field of the same entity — GCP merges them into one complete structural type.

title: ★ ADT Dispatch + Structural Inference
description: GCP combines ADT pattern matching with pure structural entity inference — the entity shape is inferred from all access patterns with zero type declarations.

title: ★ Required Field Enforcement
description: GCP distinguishes required fields from default-value and literal-type fields at construction time — missing required fields are caught as type errors with a clear message.

title: ★ Entity Literal Types
description: An entity literal type pins a field to a fixed entity value — auto-filled at construction, impossible to override.

title: ★ Tuple Literal Types
description: A tuple literal type pins a field to a fixed tuple constant — just like string/number literal types, but for compound tuple values.

title: ★ External Builder Protocol
description: GCP's __external_builder__ syntax lets runtime plugins construct typed entities — GCP verifies the type at compile time while the plugin supplies the value at runtime.
